description:
A Kubernetes developer has handed you a small collection of Deployment
    manifests that all still reference image tag “v1”.  
    Your job is to build an isolated Python environment with `pip`,
    install the exact helper libraries the team agreed on,
    then write and run a utility that automatically bumps every container
    image tag from `v1` to `v2`.

    1. Create a **virtual environment** at  
       /home/user/venvs/kubeops

    2. Inside that venv, install **exactly** the following two packages and
       no others (do **not** allow pip to upgrade/downgrade them):
       • kubernetes == 24.2.0  
       • ruamel.yaml == 0.17.21

    3. Produce a fully-pinned requirements file at  
       /home/user/venvs/kubeops/requirements.txt  
       The file must be generated with a “freeze” style command and must
       include the two exact version-pinned lines shown above.  
       Automated tests will search the file for those exact strings.

    4. Write a Python script called  
       /home/user/update_images.py  
       The script must:
       • Recursively scan the directory /home/user/manifests for “*.yaml”
         files. (The directory already exists and contains a few test
         manifests.)  
       • For every YAML document that contains a top-level  
         `kind: Deployment`, replace every container image that ends with
         “:v1” with the same repository but the tag “:v2”.  
       • Save the result to the **new** directory
         /home/user/manifests_updated (which the script must create if it
         does not already exist) using the exact same file name as the
         original.  Do **not** overwrite the source file.

    5. The script must additionally create a plain-text log file
       /home/user/update_run.log that contains **one line per file
       updated** in the following fixed format (tests are case-sensitive):

          <filename> OLD=<old_image> NEW=<new_image>

       Example line (spacing exactly as shown):

          web-deploy.yaml OLD=myapp/web:v1 NEW=myapp/web:v2

       • <filename> is only the base name, not the path  
       • <old_image> and <new_image> are the full image strings exactly as
         they appeared/now appear in the manifest  
       • Lines must be separated by UNIX newlines (`\n`).  No extra blank
         lines at the end of the file.

    6. Run the script once so that
       a) /home/user/manifests_updated contains the updated manifests, and  
       b) /home/user/update_run.log exists and follows the format above.

    7. When everything is finished, verify your work by showing:
       • The contents of /home/user/venvs/kubeops/requirements.txt  
       • An `ls -1` (one-column listing) of /home/user/manifests_updated  
       • The contents of /home/user/update_run.log

    The automated checker will confirm:

       – /home/user/venvs/kubeops is a valid virtualenv  
       – Both required packages are installed at the correct versions  
       – /home/user/venvs/kubeops/requirements.txt contains accurate
         pinned versions  
       – /home/user/manifests_updated contains manifest files whose
         `image:` fields end with “:v2”  
       – /home/user/update_run.log strictly follows the specified line
         format and lists every updated manifest exactly once.

    Hint: no cluster interaction is needed; all work is file based.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing files that the checker will rely on -->
    <initial_state>
        /home/user/manifests/            (directory, 755)
        /home/user/manifests/web-deploy.yaml
        /home/user/manifests/cache-deploy.yaml
    </initial_state>

    <web-deploy.yaml>
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: web
        image: myapp/web:v1
    </web-deploy.yaml>

    <cache-deploy.yaml>
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: cache
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: cache
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: cache
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: cache
        image: myapp/cache:v1
    </cache-deploy.yaml>

    <!-- Expected content after successful task completion -->
    <expected_directories>
        /home/user/venvs/kubeops/               (valid python venv)
        /home/user/manifests_updated/           (directory, 755)
    </expected_directories>

    <expected_files>
        /home/user/venvs/kubeops/requirements.txt
        /home/user/update_images.py
        /home/user/update_run.log
        /home/user/manifests_updated/web-deploy.yaml
        /home/user/manifests_updated/cache-deploy.yaml
    </expected_files>

    <requirements_match_strings>
        kubernetes==24.2.0
        ruamel.yaml==0.17.21
    </requirements_match_strings>

    <manifests_updated_contents>
        <!-- precise substring checker will look for these -->
        image: myapp/web:v2
        image: myapp/cache:v2
    </manifests_updated_contents>

    <log_file_regex>
        ^web-deploy.yaml OLD=myapp/web:v1 NEW=myapp/web:v2$
        ^cache-deploy.yaml OLD=myapp/cache:v1 NEW=myapp/cache:v2$
    </log_file_regex>
name: task_000000_630be072
